mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite falling unemployment, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and consumer credit indicates broad-based economic expansion that lifts corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing, or forward returns turn negative when both series rise concurrently for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects rising capacity utilization and supply-side efficiency gains that expand profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth decouples from equity returns, or SPX 20-day returns remain flat when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the consumer price index triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite CPI spikes above the threshold, or the signal produces positive forward returns in > 50% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP expands the aggregate earnings base for publicly traded companies, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Real GDP growth stagnates or contracts while SPX 60-day returns remain positive, or the historical correlation between GDP growth and forward equity returns drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and a stagnant unemployment rate indicates structural labor market imbalances that suppress wage growth and consumer purchasing power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns, or equity markets rally when payroll growth exceeds 150k while unemployment plateaus.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 0.25 percent signals the end of the zero-lower-bound environment, increasing borrowing costs for leveraged corporate balance sheets and compressing equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive despite funds rates crossing the threshold, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a rolling 3-year window.
